articles.title: Buxar Viral Video Shows Exam Paper Bundles in Rainwater; Education Department Denies Negligence - Times Now
articles.full_text:
The first spell of heavy monsoon rain in Buxar caused issues for the state education department as bundles of exam question papers got washed away by rainwater. A local resident recorded the incident and shared the video on social media, where it quickly went viral. The footage shows bundles of question papers getting soaked and floating away in the water, raising serious concerns over the safe storage and handling of confidential examination material.
The clip was shared on social media, where it quickly went viral, prompting criticism of the Education Department's handling of sensitive documents.
According to reports, the question papers were meant for Classes 9, 10 and 12 and had been sent to the Buxar Education Department. Some reports have also suggested that examination papers from nearly 167 schools may have been affected.
Department Refutes Viral Reports of Exam Paper Negligence
Meanwhile, the Bihar Education Department has labelled the online reports alleging negligence as 'baseless and misleading.' The officials have maintained that the sealed packets containing the question papers were not damaged despite the rain and that the scheduled examinations were conducted without disruption.
According to the Buxar District Education Office, the question papers and answer sheets for the quarterly exams scheduled for July 2026 were received by the district by June 27, 2026. However, as MP Higher Secondary School, Buxar, was designated as a centre for the police constable recruitment examination on June 28, 2026, the examination materials were shifted and securely stored at the Government Basic School, Buxar, to ensure their safety.
On June 30, 2026, schools across the district were collecting question papers and answer sheets. The packets for different schools were being sorted and loaded into vehicles for transportation when it suddenly started raining. The department alleged that some anti-social elements captured photos and videos during the rain and circulated them on social media with the intention of defaming the department and spreading false rumours.
It clarified that despite the sudden rainfall during the distribution and collection process, all packets containing question papers and answer sheets were safely transported to the respective schools.
